pi_items.source: Web search and multiple independent sources (IMDB, Wikipedia, library catalogs, film festivals)
Web search results confirm the documentary director is Susan Saladoff (IMDB, Film Independent, NPR, IndieWire)
Susan Saladoff is the director of the documentary; 'director' is a valid creator role for Video Recording items in Zotero (Zotero Documentation: kb:media_creator_roles)
Multiple sources confirm 2011: Sundance premiere (January 2011), HBO broadcast premiere (June 27, 2011), official release (November 1, 2011). All sources consistently cite 2011 as the year.

Library catalog entries (Berkeley Law, Ontario Public Library) list 'HBO Documentary Films' as the distributor. Web search confirms the film was produced by HBO Documentary Films and later distributed on DVD/Blu-ray through Docurama Films/New Video Group.
Web search results from Educational Media Reviews Online (EMRO) and library catalog entries confirm the runtime is 86 minutes

privileged_info_summary: Hot Coffee is a 2011 documentary directed by Susan Saladoff that analyzes tort reform in the United States judicial system. The documentary has a runtime of 86 minutes and was distributed by HBO Documentary Films. Susan Saladoff is the primary creator/director of the film.
